title: Depression review with passive suicidal ideation and safety plan

transcript.speaker: GP
transcript.text: How have things been since we last reviewed your mood?

transcript.speaker: Patient
transcript.text: Worse. I am sleeping badly and I keep thinking everyone would be better off if I did not wake up.

transcript.speaker: GP
transcript.text: Have you had thoughts of ending your life, made a plan, or taken steps to harm yourself?

transcript.speaker: Patient
transcript.text: No plan and I have not done anything. The thoughts scare me. My partner is at home and I can tell her.

transcript.speaker: GP
transcript.text: Any alcohol or drug use, access to tablets or weapons, or previous attempts?

transcript.speaker: Patient
transcript.text: No weapons. I have my usual sertraline at home. No previous attempts. I had two beers last night.

transcript.speaker: GP
transcript.text: We will increase support today. I want you to give excess tablets to your partner, use the crisis line or ED if thoughts become active, and I will call you tomorrow. We will also arrange psychology review and consider medication adjustment.

candidate_notes.note: Depression review. Mood worse, poor sleep, passive death wishes. Direct risk assessment: no active suicidal intent, no plan, no self-harm steps, no previous attempts, no weapons. Protective factor: partner at home and patient willing to tell her. Medication access discussed; advised partner hold excess tablets. Safety plan: crisis line/ED if thoughts become active. GP phone review tomorrow, psychology referral, medication review.
Depression follow-up. Patient reports worse sleep and low mood. Denies suicidal ideation. Psychology referral discussed and medication review planned.

S: Depression worse, poor sleep, passive thoughts of not waking. No plan or action. Partner at home. O: Engaged in discussion. A: Depression with passive suicidal ideation. P: Psychology referral and consider medication adjustment. Safety advice given.

Depression with passive suicidal ideation. Patient denies active intent, plan, preparatory acts, prior attempts, and weapon access. Partner at home; patient agrees to disclose worsening thoughts. Excess tablets to be held by partner. Crisis line/ED if active thoughts emerge. GP phone review tomorrow. Psychology referral and medication review planned.

CDSS mental-health risk checklist completed. Passive death wishes present; no active suicidal intent, plan, preparation, prior attempts, or weapon access. Alcohol limited to two beers last night. Protective factor: partner at home. Means-safety plan: partner to hold excess sertraline. Escalation: crisis line/ED if active SI. Follow-up call tomorrow, psychology referral, medication review.
